expected_benefit: Collapse repeated residual/bias accumulation chains when source fanout is single-consumer, avoiding intermediate F32 writes and repeated launches.
acceptance: Accept variable-length variants only when the measured fused chain beats the best same-shape unfused ADD chain, with route selection preserving the unfused fallback for high-fanout or aliasing cases.

expected_benefit: Eliminate one F32 normalized-vector materialization and one dispatch for common norm-weight application.
acceptance: Accept only with same-target, same-shape fused time below best measured unfused RMS_NORM plus MUL time.

evidence.summary: Opt-in route fired for Llama 3.2 3B Q4_K_M and reduced decode dispatches 36725 -> 33020, but t/s was flat/slightly worse: decode 40.251 -> 39.892, p512 60.997 -> 60.025. Keep disabled by default until a fused route wins directly.

expected_benefit: Reduce attention intermediate traffic and dispatch count for the common QK-softmax-V motif; likely has distinct decode and prompt implementations.
acceptance: Accept only after direct-dispatch/compiler stability is verified for attention-adjacent routes and same-run evidence shows the fused route beats the best unfused chain for each prompt/decode bucket.

expected_benefit: Fuse quantized FFN matvec outputs with GLU-style activation/gating epilogues where graph layout keeps the produced vector single-consumer.
acceptance: Accept per quant/layout/shape bucket only when fused time beats the best unfused matvec plus activation/epilogue chain and CPU-reference backend op tests cover activation edge cases.
accepted_scope: Single-column Q4_K/F32 FFN SWIGLU epilogues for split gate/up projections and packed 2x-width projections. Prompt shapes are excluded from this direct F32-RHS route family.

evidence.summary: Initial three-model slice improved 1.1%..6.3% before packed prompt MMQ routes existed. Current Phase 2a A/B after Q4/Q5/Q6 MMQ acceptance shows prompt should use the separate packed Q4_K x Q8_1 MMQ routes plus standalone SWIGLU instead; direct fused prompt is a major regression.

expected_benefit: Fuse MoE routed matvec chains with gate/activation epilogues for models where selected expert rows keep the chain local.
acceptance: Accept only for route-density and expert-count buckets proven in basket traces, with unfused fallback retained for sparse or high-fanout cases.

expected_benefit: Fold bias/add epilogue into hot matmul routes and avoid materializing the unfused matmul output.
acceptance: Accept per quant/layout/shape bucket only when measured fused time beats best measured unfused MUL_MAT plus ADD.

expected_benefit: Fold MoE expert bias and scale/gate epilogues into routed matvec work where graph layout permits.
acceptance: Accept only for measured MoE route-density buckets where fused time beats best unfused route chain.

expected_benefit: Avoid normalized/scaled Q/K vector materialization before position encoding.
acceptance: Defer route enablement until the Loom direct-dispatch binding fix is finalized and attention-adjacent backend op tests are stable.

expected_benefit: Reduce KV-cache update traffic and host-orchestration SET_ROWS overhead.
acceptance: Treat as infrastructure/fusion work, not Phase 2 tranche-1 route acceptance.

expected_benefit: Collapse MoE selection pipeline variants when model graph shape and masks match.
acceptance: Accept only after basket dataflow proves a matching motif and focused correctness covers selected MoE rows.
